title: Webull Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)
meta_title: Webull Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026) | TradingToolsHub
meta_description: Webull Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Webull (4.2★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Webull — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Webull?

Webull is a zero-commission trading platform founded in 2017 that lets you trade stocks, options, ETFs, futures, and cryptocurrencies—all without paying per-trade commissions. The company operates under NASDAQ ticker BULL and is headquartered in New York. With a 4.2/5 rating on TradingToolsHub, Webull is built specifically for active traders and options traders who want professional-grade charting tools and extended market hours without the high fees of traditional brokers. The platform offers a free Standard tier and an optional Premium subscription at $3.99 per month, making it one of the most affordable ways to access institutional-quality trading technology.

How to Create Your Webull Account

Creating a Webull account takes approximately 10-15 minutes from start to finish. Here's the exact process:

  • Visit webull.com and click "Sign Up." Choose whether you're opening an individual account or an IRA account. Note: Webull does not support SEP IRAs, inherited IRAs, trust accounts, or custodial accounts (529s/UTMAs), so stick to individual or IRA for now.
  • Enter your email and password. Set a secure password with at least 8 characters, one uppercase letter, one number, and one special character. Enable two-factor authentication immediately—Webull sends a code to your phone to verify login attempts.
  • Provide personal information. Full name, date of birth, Social Security number, and residential address. This is required for regulatory compliance (KYC).
  • Link your bank account. Webull needs your bank details to verify your identity and enable funding. You can use ACH transfers (free but slow—3-5 business days) or wire transfers (fees apply but instant). Most new users use ACH.
  • Complete identity verification. Upload a photo ID (driver's license or passport) and Webull's system verifies it using automated facial recognition. This typically completes in minutes, though some submissions take up to 48 hours.
  • Answer investment experience questions. These determine your risk profile and which products you can access (options require approval, for example). Answer honestly—too aggressive answers may trigger manual review.
  • Deposit funds. Transfer at least $0 (yes, you can start with zero and use paper trading to practice). Webull does not require a minimum deposit for Standard accounts, though options trading requires $2,000 minimum account equity.
  • Accept the terms and go live. Once your account is approved (typically within 24 hours), you can begin trading during extended hours immediately—or wait for regular market hours if you're new to trading.

Setting Up Webull for the First Time

When you log in for the first time, Webull's dashboard can feel overwhelming. Here's how to configure it for your trading style:

1. Choose Your Dashboard Layout

Webull offers multiple dashboard templates: "Trader" (focused on charts and watchlists), "Investor" (portfolio and news), and "Crypto" (if you trade digital assets). Start with "Trader" and customize your widgets. Pin your watchlist to the top-left and drag your main chart to the center.

2. Import or Create Watchlists

Webull lets you sync watchlists from ThinkorSwim or E*TRADE if you're migrating. If you're new, start by creating three watchlists: (1) "Stock Screener Results" for systematic scans, (2) "Options Plays" for positions you're tracking, and (3) "News Flow" for stocks in the news. You can sync up to 5,000 tickers across watchlists.

3. Configure Alerts and Notifications

Go to Settings → Alerts and enable push notifications for price targets, volume spikes, and earnings dates. Set alerts for specific conditions (e.g., "notify me when XYZ breaks above $150") rather than static price points—Webull's alerting is reactive, not predictive. For serious traders, enable SMS alerts for critical positions.

4. Calibrate Level 2 Market Data (Premium Users Only)

If you subscribe to Webull Premium ($3.99/month), you unlock Level 2 quotes showing market depth and real-time order book flow. Customize your Level 2 view to show the bid-ask spread prominently—this is essential for options traders timing entries.

5. Link Your Broker (if applicable)

Webull has deep integrations with major brokers. If you're a Webull-only trader, skip this. But if you hold positions at Robinhood, Interactive Brokers, or Tastytrade, you can connect them to Webull's research tools and track all positions in one dashboard via the "Broker Connection" feature.

6. Test Paper Trading Mode

Before risking real money, spend at least one week in Paper Trading mode with $100,000 of fake cash. This is not a demo—it's a real, live sandbox that uses actual market data. Practice your first trade, place a covered call, and test your order types (market, limit, stop-loss). Webull's paper trading is ranked #1 by StockBrokers.com for 2026.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Commission-Free Trading Across All Asset Classes

Webull charges zero commission on stocks, ETFs, options, and over 50 cryptocurrencies. Compare this to traditional brokers: E*TRADE charges $0 per stock trade but $0.65 per options contract; TD Ameritrade charges $0 but limits some advanced features to Premium members. Webull's free tier includes everything—no hidden fees per contract leg.

2. Extended Hours Trading (4AM–8PM ET)

While most brokers limit pre-market and after-hours trading to PRO accounts, Webull includes this free in Standard. Trade earnings announcements at 8:05 AM before the market opens, or exit positions between 4 PM and 8 PM. Overnight trading is available on select securities—check your stock's extended hours eligibility before placing an order.

3. 56 Technical Indicators and Replay Mode

Webull's charting engine includes advanced tools like Ichimoku clouds, volume-weighted moving averages, and Heikin-Ashi candles. But the hidden gem is Replay Mode: play back any historical time period in real-time (e.g., replay March 2020's market crash at normal speed and practice shorting it). This feature is free and invaluable for backtesting strategies without simulation bias.

4. Paper Trading Ranked #1 by Industry Benchmarks

Webull's paper trading environment was named the best in 2026 by StockBrokers.com. Unlike Robinhood's basic paper trading (which is poorly maintained), Webull's sandbox executes orders in real-time with real market data and full slippage simulation. Use it to test earnings swing strategies, covered call stacks, and iron condors without burning capital.

5. AI Analysis and Order Flow Intelligence

Webull's built-in AI analyzes price patterns and alerts you to unusual order flow—buy walls, sell walls, and sudden accumulation. This data comes from their order flow analysis module, which shows institutional-sized orders before they hit the tape. Free in Standard; enhanced alerts in Premium.

6. Mobile App with Full Feature Parity

Webull's iOS and Android apps are not stripped-down versions—they include charting, options chains, Level 2 (Premium), and order routing. You can execute a full straddle from your phone while waiting for an earnings announcement. This is crucial for mobile traders who refuse to be chained to a desktop.

7. Built-In News Feed and Social Features

Webull includes a live news feed with earnings calendars, SEC filings, and insider transactions. The social features let you follow other traders' positions and discussion threads (think Twitter for traders). This is useful for monitoring unusual activity, but disable notifications—the signal-to-noise ratio is poor.

Webull Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Standard (Free) — Best for Most New Traders

$0/month. Includes commission-free trading, extended hours, paper trading, 56 indicators, news feed, mobile app, AI analysis, and order flow analysis. This is genuinely free—no hidden costs, no data fees, no surprise platform charges. If you're starting out or swing trading (holding positions overnight), Standard covers everything you need.

Webull Premium — Only If You Trade Options or Short-Term

$3.99/month (or $40/year if paid annually). Adds: Level 2 market depth, lower margin rates (from 8% to 6% on borrowed funds), advanced screener filters, and a few proprietary indicators. At $40/year, Premium is 80% cheaper than Robinhood Gold ($239/year) and includes more features. Only buy Premium if you: (1) trade options and need to see the order book to time limit orders, (2) use 4+ technical indicators at once, or (3) borrow money on margin frequently. If you're a buy-and-hold investor, skip it.

Recommended Tier by Trader Type:

  • Beginner (first 3 months): Standard. Paper trade for 2-4 weeks, then move to small real positions. Premium is unnecessary until you hit options.
  • Active Options Trader: Premium. Level 2 and margin rate reductions pay for themselves on 4-5 multi-leg orders per month.
  • Day Trader (scalping stocks): Standard is fine. Webull's order execution is fast enough for 1-5 minute intraday moves without paying extra.
  • Swing/Position Trader: Standard. Extended hours alone save you thousands per year versus Robinhood or TD Ameritrade.
  • Crypto Trader: Standard. Webull's crypto trading is free on Standard, but note: you cannot transfer coins to external wallets—they must stay on Webull's platform. This is a major limitation vs. Kraken or Coinbase if self-custody is important to you.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Webull

1. Use Replay Mode to Backtest Earnings Strategies

Pick a stock with historical volatility (e.g., Tesla, Nvidia). Jump to an earnings date from 6 months ago, enable Replay Mode, and watch the candles form in real-time. Place hypothetical straddles and strangles at different strike prices and note your average win rate. This costs zero dollars and is faster than backtesting software.

2. Layer Your Alerts by Timeframe

Set price alerts on support/resistance levels identified on daily charts, but set volume alerts on 15-minute intraday charts. Webull lets you stack alerts—when a stock breaks a daily resistance level AND volume spikes on the 15-minute, you get a double-alert notification. This filters out false breakouts.

3. Exploit Extended Hours for Earnings Plays

Most brokers won't let you trade until 9:30 AM, but Webull's 4 AM open lets you front-run the bell on big earnings. Place your position at 4:15 AM before the market wakes up, then exit at 9:35 AM after the rush. The spreads are worse in pre-market (3-5 cents wider than regular hours), but you get first-mover advantage on liquidity events.

4. Monitor Order Flow Before Heavy Positions

Before entering a 10,000+ share position, check Webull's order flow data. A wall of buy orders below the market usually means institutional support—a good sign for entering long. A wall of sell orders above the market signals distribution and means be cautious. This data is free in Standard and is a macro-level edge over traders without it.

5. Export Your Paper Trading Results Weekly

Webull's paper trading records every trade, including entry price, exit price, and P&L. Export your results every Friday (Settings → Account → Export Trades) and track your win rate by strategy. You'll notice patterns: maybe your covered calls work 65% of the time but your straddles work 45% of the time. This data-driven approach beats emotion-driven trading.

6. Avoid the Social Feed; Use Screeners Instead

Webull's social features are noisy and full of pump-and-dump schemes. Disable notifications and instead use the built-in screener to find catalysts: earnings dates, unusual volume spikes, and insider buying. The screener is basic compared to Finviz or Trade Ideas, but it costs nothing and is fast enough for day traders.

7. Link Webull to Your Tax Software Early

Webull exports CSV trade reports compatible with TurboTax and TaxAct. Export your full year's trades in December and import them into tax software immediately—don't wait until April. A single options spread with 3 legs generates 3 tax lots; Webull's records are comprehensive but only if you export them on time.

Common Webull Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: Options Approval Stuck or Denied

Webull approves options trading within 24 hours for most users, but some are stuck in "pending" for days. If this happens: (1) double-check your answers to investment experience questions—if you answered "0 years" of options experience, Webull may auto-deny Level 2 approval, (2) contact Webull Support via in-app chat (they respond in 10+ minutes, not ideal), or (3) call 1-833-923-9355. Options approval cannot be appealed—you'll need to reapply with different answers if denied.

Issue 2: Margin Call Due to PFOF Slippage

Webull generates ~50% of revenue from payment for order flow (PFOF)—they sell your order data to market makers. On illiquid stocks or early-morning orders, this results in execution 1-3 cents worse than the quoted price. If you're using margin and buying low-float stocks (like Nvidia's cousin, a penny stock), you may get a surprise margin call when your purchase price is 3 cents worse than expected. Fix: Limit margin buying to highly liquid ETFs and mega-cap stocks (SPY, QQQ, AAPL). Avoid penny stocks on margin entirely.

Issue 3: Crypto Cannot Leave Webull

Webull does not support cryptocurrency withdrawals to external wallets. If you buy Bitcoin on Webull, you must sell it on Webull—you cannot move it to a cold wallet or another exchange. If self-custody is critical to your strategy (especially for long-term hodlers), use Kraken or Coinbase instead. Webull crypto is only suitable for trading, not long-term holding.

Issue 4: Customer Support is Ranked Last Among Brokers

Webull's customer support is its Achilles heel. They're ranked 0.92/10 on support satisfaction with hold times exceeding 10 minutes. If you need real-time help (e.g., an order didn't fill correctly), Webull is not the right choice. Use email support for non-urgent issues and expect 24-48 hour responses. If you need instant help, TD Ameritrade's ThinkorSwim or Interactive Brokers have better support.

Is Webull Worth It? Our Verdict

Webull is worth it if you're an active options trader, extended-hours trader, or mobile-first trader who doesn't need reliable customer support. The free tier is genuinely feature-complete—Level 2 and margin savings add maybe $5-10/month in value, not worth paying $40/year unless you trade options multiple times per week. The platform's extended hours (4 AM–8 PM), paper trading (ranked #1), and zero commission structure beat Robinhood, E*TRADE, and most free brokers. However, Webull is not suitable for long-term crypto holders (coins can't leave the platform), traders needing institutional-grade support, or anyone uncomfortable with PFOF-driven execution. Start with the free tier, paper trade for 2-4 weeks, then commit to real money only after you've verified your strategy works. For detailed feature comparisons, see our full Webull review and Webull vs Robinhood comparison.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
